es.example: Hace dos años vivía en Madrid.
es.exampleTranslation: Two years ago I was living in Madrid.

it.example: Due anni fa vivevo a Milano.
it.exampleTranslation: Two years ago I was living in Milan.

bridge.note: Both languages tell time with 'to make' — hacer and fare, the same verb — but in mirror positions: Spanish puts hace BEFORE the span, Italian puts fa AFTER it. Same bone, opposite skeleton. Spanish once had the postposed form too: the archaic 'años ha' sits exactly where fa does.

interference.es_to_it: The mirror bites reliably: Spanish speakers produce 'fa due anni'. The span comes first: due anni fa.
interference.it_to_es: Italians produce 'dos años hace'. Spanish wants hace first: hace dos años — the postposed order survives only in the archaic 'años ha'.

review.notes: Submitted by Joe 2026-07-14. Word-order mirror is the payload.

cue: How do you say how long ago something happened?
